261.reason: Same issue: errorFlag is set but the token is not properly handled; the program does not stop and may create incorrect tokens.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Same issue: errorFlag is set but the token is not properly handled; the program does not stop and may create incorrect tokens.

216.reason: The logic for handling non-escape character constants is flawed. It reads the character, then immediately reads the next character (expecting it to be the closing quote) into tokenContent without checking if it IS the closing quote. This consumes the closing quote and potentially the next character, causing subsequent parsing errors. It should check if the current char is the closing quote or handle the single character case correctly before reading ahead for the closing quote.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The logic for handling non-escape character constants is flawed. It reads the character, then immediately reads the next character (expecting it to be the closing quote) into tokenContent without checking if it IS the closing quote. This consumes the closing quote and potentially the next character, causing subsequent parsing errors. It should check if the current char is the closing quote or handle the single character case correctly before reading ahead for the closing quote.

218.reason: This read consumes the character expected to be the closing quote, but it's added to tokenContent in the next line only if the loop condition fails or if it's not handled correctly. Actually, the next line adds `curChar` to tokenContent unconditionally. If `curChar` was the closing quote `'`, it gets added to the content, and then the loop `while (curChar != -1 && (char)curChar != '\'')` will terminate immediately because `curChar` is `'`. Then the `if (curChar == '\'')` check passes, and another `'` is added. This results in incorrect token content like `'a''` instead of `'a'`. More critically, if the char is not the closing quote, it reads one more char which might be the closing quote, but that closing quote is NOT added to tokenContent here, it's left for the while loop. The logic is tangled. A correct implementation should read the character inside the quotes (handling escape sequences if necessary), then expect and consume the closing quote.

More critically, if the char is not the closing quote, it reads one more char which might be the closing quote, but that closing quote is NOT added to tokenContent here, it's left for the while loop. The logic is tangled. A correct implementation should read the character inside the quotes (handling escape sequences if necessary), then expect and consume the closing quote.

220.reason: [This incorrectly assumes a char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and blindly appends the next character as the closing quote, even if it's not a quote. It should only read one character for the content and then expect a closing single quote. This causes multi-character or malformed char literals to be parsed incorrectly, consuming extra characters beyond the intended char constant.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line incorrectly adds the character read above (which might be the closing quote or the next character after the value) to the token content.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  This line incorrectly consumes the closing quote or subsequent characters as part of the token content for simple char literals like 'a'. It should check if the current character is the closing quote instead of blindly appending and reading ahead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This incorrectly assumes a char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and blindly appends the next character as the closing quote, even if it's not a quote. It should only read one character for the content and then expect a closing single quote. This causes multi-character or malformed char literals to be parsed incorrectly, consuming extra characters beyond the intended char constant.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line incorrectly assumes a char literal always has exactly one character between quotes and blindly appends the next character as the closing quote, even if it's not a quote. It should check that the next character is a single quote to properly form a valid char constant like 'a'. Instead, it reads one char, then immediately reads and appends another (which may be anything), breaking parsing of subsequent tokens.]

221.reason: This line advances the cursor past the closing quote (if it was consumed above) or further into the source, causing the lexer to miss the end of the character constant and merge subsequent tokens.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  This line advances the cursor past the closing quote (if it was consumed above) or further into the source, causing the lexer to miss the end of the character constant and merge subsequent tokens.

223.reason: [This loop is unreachable in the normal case because the above block already consumed two characters after the opening quote, including what should have been the closing quote. If the char literal is well-formed like 'a', this loop won't run, but the closing quote was already consumed and appended incorrectly as part of the content. If the literal is malformed or contains escape sequences, this logic fails completely.]
